parityMeta.describes: PA-FIX (S4 \b fidelity, engine-mirror) buildAdjustmentDiff word-boundary parity — a reduce_support diff over a day whose NAME is an English template token glued directly to a CJK char ('legs训练', no separator). summarizeSupportState formats that day name through the engine's local formatTemplateName mirror; JS \b is ASCII-word-only and DOES find a boundary between 'legs' and '训', so the residual-English-word guard fires and the day label falls back to '未指定训练日'. An unfixed NSRegularExpression (ICU) \b treats CJK as a word char, finds NO boundary, and would leak the raw 'legs训练'. Existing build-diff-cases-v1 keeps English tokens space/dash-delimited, so this divergence was unexercised; this fixture pins it. Generated from src/engines/programAdjustmentEngine.ts; the Swift port (with \b spelled as explicit ASCII look-arounds) compute-asserts the ProgramAdjustmentDiff == golden.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: No clock — buildAdjustmentDiff is clockless. Synthetic template; userId-free; no email/token. The 'legs训练' day name exercises the ASCII word-boundary fix only.

cases.label: reduce_support over a day whose name is an English template word glued to CJK ('legs训练') → ASCII-\b lock: summarizeSupportState's formatDayTemplateName hits the residual-English-word guard and returns fallback '未指定训练日', NOT the raw 'legs训练'

draft.title: 推 A 下周实验调整
draft.summary: 天名为英文直接黏 CJK 的辅助层调整。
